reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof first, then headless-Chrome real download + real inference on the index page via CDP driver, mobile+desktop overflow + console/network checks, chroma-field visual review)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference proven BEFORE building: an ORT-web@1.21.0 WASM harness (headless Chrome, no GPU) loaded ddcolor-tiny-fp16.onnx and ran it — session input 'input' [1,3,512,512] f32, output 'output' [1,2,512,512] f32. On a mountain landscape the predicted chroma is strong and correct (a-std 7.38, b-std 20.5; reconstructed image shows blue sky, green forest, snowy peaks — screenshot reviewed); on a faded studio portrait it is honestly muted (a-std 1.18, b-std 5.17). transformers.js 3.7.5/4.2 registers no `ddcolor` class and no image-colorization pipeline task, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module Worker (isolated per-worker pin, like scene-text-detection). Headless page build-verify (CDP driver): fresh profile → Download (~130 MB) → ready → Colourise produced real output, inference 5738 ms + composite 56 ms on WASM, 0 console errors, 0 network failures.
rubric.notes: DDColor predicts CHROMA only; luminance is the source image's, so structure is never altered — the model can only get colour right or wrong.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed: output tensor dims [1,2,512,512] read back at runtime; the ab chroma is upsampled (bilinear) and recombined with the full-resolution L, then Lab→RGB. Semantic check: colourful scenes yield high chroma std (landscape a7.4/b20.5) while near-neutral subjects yield low std — the output tracks content, not a constant. The raw predicted chroma field is shown in 'See inside' at a fixed mid-luminance so the invented colour is visible alone.
rubric.notes: image-to-image task template (dense per-pixel output grounded in the input) fits: colourised RGB derived from predicted ab + original L.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance names the exact model (edgetools/ddcolor, linked), task (image-to-image / colorization), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), input [1,3,512,512] grayscale-RGB / output [1,2,512,512] ab shapes, dtype (fp16 weights, fp32 I/O via keep_io_types → plain WASM EP, no WebGPU), size (~130 MB) and licence (Apache-2.0), and honestly notes transformers.js has no DDColor class. inference-off-main-thread = pass (new Worker + worker.js). No invented API.
rubric.notes: fp16 weights but fp32 I/O boundary keeps op support maximal on the WASM execution provider — verified running on a no-GPU runner.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads through createModelLoader. The worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage (ddcolor-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/edgetools/ddcolor/' and the shared loader auto-inits on a returning visit (no Download button); the per-model clear-cache control works. First-visit driver run showed the honest Download (~130 MB) state, streaming progress, then ready.
rubric.notes: No hand-rolled Load button; the 4 ladder pages share the same modelId → shared cache → auto-init.

rubric.evidence: loads-clean = pass (0 console errors on load, index + ladder pages). Download progress streams from the worker (content-length) into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Status uses role=status aria-live=polite. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner.
rubric.notes: A 512×512 pass is a few seconds on single-threaded WASM; the UI says so up front and the button is disabled with a working status while running.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised and labelled + keyboard-operable: the rights-safe example gallery (buttons with aria-pressed + attribution), the drop-zone + file input, the Colourise / Download buttons, the colour-intensity and output-resolution <input type=range> sliders, and the before/after reveal (a native <input type=range> with aria-valuetext '% colourised', keyboard-operable, not a mouse-only widget). The wild page adds an accessible segmented toggle (grayscale→guess / guess→truth) with aria-pressed.
rubric.notes: Sliders re-run a single bounded forward pass off-thread.

rubric.evidence: All colour, the chroma field, and the match score come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. Distinct, content-correct results per image (vivid landscape vs muted portrait) prove the output tracks the real model, not a placeholder. The wild page's colour-match score is computed from the model output vs the image's real colours in CIE-Lab.
rubric.notes: Uploads with no ground truth honestly disable the score (needs the original colours).

rubric.evidence: images-have-alt = pass, buttons-have-names = pass, live-status-region = pass. Canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-label (grayscale input / colourised output / predicted chroma field); the reveal slider is a labelled native range with aria-valuetext; gallery thumbnails carry the source/licence in their accessible name and on-screen caption. Status is role=status aria-live=polite. :focus-visible styled on the reveal + drop-zone.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied for the accessible comparison slider and canvas alternatives (see guidanceConsulted).

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop = pass, responsive-mobile = pass. CDP driver measured horizontal overflow = 0 px at BOTH desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 on the index page. Layout uses flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0 two-column (image | controls) that stacks on mobile; the gallery is an auto-fill minmax grid; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto; the reveal uses logical properties + touch-action:pan-y. Light + dark inherit design-system variables.
rubric.notes: No 100vh traps; the reveal handle + range use inset-inline logical positioning.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense output composite (bilinear ab upsample + full-resolution Lab→RGB, the systemic hotspot invariant 15 warns about) run in the module Worker; the colourised, grayscale, and chroma images are built with OffscreenCanvas and returned as transferred ImageBitmaps (zero-copy). The main thread only draws bitmaps. Measured composite 56 ms in the worker (off the main thread), so no main-thread long task during interaction.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes — the per-pixel Lab work is off the main thread and transferred, not cloned.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible range slider input keyboard labels aria-valuetext, canvas role img alt, live status region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer native <label>/native controls; a range input is keyboard-operable and announces its value (add aria-valuetext for a human-readable position); give informative canvases role=img + a descriptive name; use a single polite live region for status; style :focus-visible.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the before/after divider is a native <input type=range> with aria-valuetext ('N% colourised'); every canvas has role=img + a descriptive aria-label; a single role=status aria-live=polite reports progress/results; :focus-visible rings on the reveal, drop-zone, and gallery.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two column image + controls no horizontal overflow flexbox intrinsic sizing logical properties
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> + min-inline-size:0 so wide flex items shrink instead of overflowing; size media with inline-size:100% block-size:auto; use logical properties; auto-fill/minmax grids for galleries.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — image/controls columns use flex:1 1 300px with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; the example gallery is a repeat(auto-fill,minmax()) grid; canvases and the reveal are inline-size:100% block-size:auto. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move image inference and dense per-pixel post-processing off main thread web worker transfer
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (inference + dense pixel loops) into a worker; transfer buffers/ImageBitmaps instead of cloning.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — ONNX inference and the whole Lab→RGB composite (the dense-output hotspot) run in a module worker; the colourised/grayscale/chroma images are transferred back as ImageBitmaps. No blocking main-thread pixel loop during interaction.

openQuestions: DDColor-tiny predicts chroma at a fixed 512×512; a follow-up could tile large images for sharper chroma placement on high-resolution scans.
A WebGPU ORT-web EP path would cut the ~5 s WASM pass substantially — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a 'colour-transfer / palette control' wild demo that nudges the predicted chroma toward a chosen palette while keeping the model's spatial colour assignment.
Add a live-camera ladder demo (lib/capture-ux.js, user-initiated) that colourises a grayscale-filtered webcam frame in near-real-time.

summary: New demo: on-device image COLORIZATION via DDColor-tiny (edgetools/ddcolor, Apache-2.0) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module worker, with the dense Lab→RGB composite off the main thread and transferred as ImageBitmaps. Runnability was proven first in headless Chrome (real ab chroma, vivid on a landscape, honestly muted on a faded portrait); the index + 4 ladder pages (basics / practical restore+download / wild AI-vs-reality score / multi-model deblur→colourise) were then build-verified with real download + inference, an accessible before/after reveal, cached auto-init, clean console, and 0 px overflow on mobile+desktop. Sample images come from the rights-safe media library with attribution.
